entry.title: Centro de custo, lançamento sem orçamento.
entry.content:
Boa tarde pessoal,

Estou com um centro de custo que realizou 3k em uma conta de viagem e o mesmo não tinha orçamento.

Queria entender em que momento o SAP deixou isso passar? Pois temos travas.

entry.title: Re: Centro de custo, lançamento sem orçamento.
entry.content: Que fez*

entry.title: Re: SQL Server 2022 not available in drop down
entry.content: same problem here, which driver should i download ? thanks

entry.title: Re: How can we see the commercial metrics from the instance level?
entry.content:
Hi Erwin,

Great question.

Could you please confirm whether 'Technical Allocation' has been configured for this service (PostgreSQL)? This is a precondition so the tool knows which Technical Metric to use as distribution key to allocate the cost to the individual instances.

To configure the Technical Allocation, go to the 'Billing Verification' > Your service. In the table with Commercial Metrics, look for the column 'Technical Allocation' (might be hidden) and set one of the Technical Metrics as its value.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation (trial) instance could n...
entry.content:
hi all, 

seems that SAP has fixed the error.

Right now I was able to create a SAP Build Process Automation application in my trial account.

I have changed nothing in my trial account.

@SAP: thanks for solving the issue !!!

entry.title: Re: Facing Subscription Challenges in SAP Build Process A...
entry.content:
hi all, 

seems that SAP has fixed the error.

Right now I was able to create a SAP Build Process Automation application in my trial account.

I have changed nothing in my trial account.

entry.title: Re: No Userexits in SUSG/SCMON Data
entry.content:
Sorry, @Sandra_Rossi  I may have been not precise enough 🙂

It was like a more generic question, but let's take the original topic example... Let's imagine EXIT_SAPLLOI1_002 has been executed from standard program. You explained that the function module EXIT_SAPLLOI1_002 call would be traced in SUSG but not the include ZXLOIU02 (which is included within the function module).

The question is, the standard SAP Custom Code Migration app is able to remove unused custom code from one system by creating a deletion transport request so we don't have to bother with unused custom code remediation. That seems great! But since the include ZXLOIU02 would not appear in SUSG, how does the application know this include should not be considered as unused?

Hope it's clearer now 🙂

Thank you.

Olivier

entry.title: Re: Can you pass sy-subrc as parameter in class constructor?
entry.content: Personally, I'd use the intermediate variable.  sy-subrc's value gets changed so easily and so often.  Even doing pass by value as you are, maybe sy-subrc's value is actually getting changed by the start of the call of the constructor even before the parameter is assigned.  If you really need a stable sy-subrc value, then copy it to a local variable as you are doing.  That's the only way to assure it stays at that particular value state.

entry.title: Persistence of CDS Views in HANA Database Local Table
entry.content:
Dear All, 

Is there an option to persist CDS view data in the HANA database when HANA DB is being used as a middleware or data warehouse?

In my scenario, I have legacy data stored in HANA DB and OLTP data in S/4HANA. I would like to create a combined view of both datasets and expose it to SAC. The goal is to improve performance by avoiding direct consumption of the CDS view in SAC.

In my case, a custom SQL view (ZSQL) has been created in S/4HANA using CDS. How can this view be accessed or persisted in a separate HANA database that is acting as a middleware or data warehouse?
I have two HANA databases: one serving OLAP purposes (data warehouse/middleware) and the other being the S/4HANA OLTP system.

entry.title: SRM login problem
entry.content: No switch to HTTPS occurred, so it is not secure to send a password Logon with URL parameter not possible; logon cookie

entry.title: CCM create intercompany cost distribution / post vendor credit memo against SO billing docs q-tities
entry.content:
Hello,

I have two questions, posting them together as wondering if 1. can be used for the purpose of 2. or not: 

1. Is there somewhere to read about the CCM contract type "create intercompany cost distribution".

2. Is there a way to post a vendor credit memo against SO billing docs quantities, so the value of the vendor credit memo is spread over proportionally to these quantities. The result should be visible in FI, so FI can trace it to the sales, the value of the credit memo once posted should contribute to the gross margin.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: CMS SSL config and thick clients
entry.content: Correct. It is not good practice to copy certificates to other places, even though it will work.

entry.title: Re: What's New in SAP Reference Architecture? [Spring Edition]
entry.content: @Kelly_Westfall Hi Kelly, thanks for reaching out! We provide our cross- and industry-specific Business Capability Model in the Reference Catalogue to SAP LeanIX user. Here you can get a first glimpse (free version). On top of this, you can also take a look at the SAP Signavio Process Explorer and navigate to "Capabilities" directly. In SAP Signavio Process Explorer, you can also find Solution Capabilities, when selecting an industry as entry point. Feel free to reach out to me if you need more insights.

Thanks again 🙂 !!!

Regards,
Amit

entry.title: Re: Access Credential Storage API using Python
entry.content:
Hello,

We are using the CredStore service and it was working fine until some days back, but recently we have started facing the below error: 

ERR jwetoken.deserialize(response.text, key=private_key)

ERR File "/home/vcap/deps/0/python/lib/python3.11/site-packages/jwcrypto/jwe.py", line 550, in deserialize

ERR raise InvalidJWEData('Invalid format', repr(e)) from e

ERR jwcrypto.jwe.InvalidJWEData: Invalid format {KeyError('iv')}


Not sure if something has changed or missed at our end, any help/suggestions is highly appreciated. 

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Depreciation expense decreasing the budget loaded in...
entry.content: Hi Mick, Thank you for your reply. We still have some doubts as below - Can we still remove the wbs element from the asset master (time-dependent data) when there are already posted transactions for the asset? we noticed that the status of wbs elements are still "released". If these are "closed" would depreciation posting still post to the wbs element? can you help to check if wbs element is assigned only to the AuC and not to the final asset, we would assume there will be no issue after settlement and depreciation posting. And link is established re: Project/WBS Element > AuC > Final Asset.

entry.title: Re: Error solving
entry.content:
This error is occurred due to missing GL assignment is OBYC transaction code for the transaction WRX.

GR/IR clearing GL account is assigned to WRX.

Account determination for entry 1110 WRX not possible, here 1110 is the valuation class and WRX is the transaction.

Assign the GL account for the valuation class 1110 for the transaction WRX using the transaction code OBYC to solve the error. Transport request will be captured for the configuration done.

entry.title: Re: CDC B2B Organization Management - Policy Catalog - Po...
entry.content:
Hello,

This is actually a pretty common situation after migrating the B2B Policy Catalog.

The "PolicyID needs to be defined" message you’re seeing is more about UI completeness — it doesn’t necessarily stop your processes from running, especially if the backend is still recognizing the original IDs from before the migration. So technically, yes, you could continue without defining new Policy IDs for now.

However, it’s strongly recommended to manually define a Policy ID at this point, ideally using the old IDs you have documented. This helps future-proof your setup, especially if future patches, upgrades, or API calls start relying on visible Policy IDs. Defining them now avoids surprises later.

You can edit the policy, assign the old ID into the Policy ID field, save it, and reactivate the policy. Processes shouldn’t be affected if you stick to the original ID values.

SAP Help doesn't document this exact post-migration behavior in detail, but the general reference is here:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_TECHNOLOGY_PLATFORM/b2b_policies_manage

(You can search for "Policy Catalog migration" and "Policy Custom ID" for related topics.)

In summary:
You can live with old IDs temporarily, but defining them now is strongly recommended to avoid future technical issues.

Regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Alumni Experience- 1H2025 Release
entry.content: @Gopinath - I have the same license questions as above.  Do customers need to account for additional Onboarding licenses for their alumni users?

entry.title: Re: CMS SSL config and thick clients

entry.title: Re: ISD-Input service distributor for distributing GST to multiple business places - In S4 Hana
entry.content:
Dear Prasad, 

Thanks for sharing the detailed flow. 

We have multiple company codes in a single client. For testing purposes, only one company code's turnover data is maintained in J_1IG_ISD_TO_V [Ex. CC-1000]. We have posted a test document in ISD business place for a different entity [Ex.CC-2000]. Now if i try to execute the J1IG_ISDN for CC-2000, it's taking the turnover value from CC-1000 instead of giving an error. Have you come across such issue? Or how fix this issue?

Regards, 

Shiva.

entry.title: Re: SAP IAS Federated Access AWS Cognito
entry.content:
Hi Julius,

I'm not very familiar with the currently landscape of this client, as far as I've know, they are introducing SAP BTP solutions for their customers very recently. But what I know is that they are using AWS solutions for hosting their systems, that's why they want to integrate AWS Cognito with SAP IAS avoiding a new login screen and user management as well.

I've seen some tutorial at sap developers tutorial page but I've only found out the steps to integrate Microsoft Azure (Entra ID) and SAP IAS, but nothing about AWS Cognito. I tried to follow those steps but I'm not sure if it was done right, that's why I'm asking for some guidance/advice and how to proceed it.

In my SAP BTP trial Account, the Workzone and IAS trust are done automatically when the booster completes but I'm kinda lost with the procedure between SAP IAS an AWS Cognito.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to create hierarchy variables in Datas...
entry.content:
Hi, yes, you cannot use hierarchies in model-variable.

Only as input-control or story filter. There you can add hierarchy, but not in model-variable. 

The hierarchy is model is missing. Same issue for restricted measures with hierarchy. 

Martin

entry.title: Re: Learners accessing their survey answers
entry.content: Outside of admins sharing survey report responses with the users, you can submit or upvote for this enhancement, see similar: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/313986 (https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/326081).

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Stock Room Management - Fiori Application...
entry.content:
Hi @pat_pillay,

Good question, this is something many are dealing with when moving from LE-WM to Stock Room Management in S/4HANA.

Currently, SAP hasn’t published a dedicated Fiori apps list just for Stock Room Management because Stock Room is a functional subset of LE-WM, mainly intended for basic warehouse operations. As a result, many standard LE-WM Fiori apps are not officially adapted or enhanced for Stock Room use.

You can check:

  • SAP Note 2882809 for Stock Room limitations.

  • SAP Fiori Apps Library (link here) — but you’ll need to search and validate manually.

  • Stock Room Management guide on SAP Help Portal.

In practice, most teams just create SAP GUI transactions as Fiori tiles to make it work nicely in the Launchpad.

Let me know if you find this helpful.

Regards,

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Flexible workflow table details
entry.content: Thanks a lot. Just missing table to find "Workflow Steps", for example Purchasing group of purchase order is XXXX net amount of purchase order is less than or equal to 1.000,00EUR

entry.title: How SAP Global Procurement Runs SAP: A Category Management Success Story
entry.content:
At SAP, we practice what we preach. Our Global Procurement Office (GPO) leads by example, embracing our own innovative solutions. The GPO has fully integrated SAP Ariba solutions – including our AI-assisted SAP Ariba Category Management solution – into SAP’s procurement processes from start to finish. By using our own tools, we're not just talking about innovation – we're living

In a recent webinar with Procurement Leaders on best practices for leveraging AI in category management, Nouh Abed Asaad, Chief Operating Officer at SAP's GPO, discussed why he and his team are proactively implementing digital tools across procurement, with an emphasis on AI.

 

Exploiting Technology to Streamline Processes and Meet Corporate Objectives

The GPO uses SAP technology to expedite procurement processes, foster close collaboration with stakeholders, and ensure alignment with corporate objectives.

Throughout the webinar, Asaad highlighted the power of digital tools to help break down data silos and build bridges across the organization. "In the past, everyone had their PowerPoint presentation, Excel files, emails, and his or her own story,” he said. “With our implementation of SAP Ariba Category Management, our goal was to bring everything to one standard to show the business units what they get when they work with procurement."

This unified approach isn't just about consolidation; it's about enhancing the decision-making process. Asaad explained, "We're bringing actionable insights and additional data to our category strategy definition, not only connecting our own data and making sure it's accurate and correct but also enriching it with external market data to make better decisions to improve our category."

Asaad also emphasized how procurement plays a crucial role in meeting real-world business needs, particularly in sustainability efforts. "We have a clear commitment toour net-zero strategy,” he said. “Our scope 3 emissions are 70-75% of our total emissions. Without procurement, you will never meet your sustainability targets."

SAP technology has been pivotal in helping the GPO achieve key corporate objectives, from data-driven decision-making to sustainability goals.

SAP Ariba Category Management with AI
The GPO operates in three regional hubs – the Americas, EMEA, and APJ – across 40 locations worldwide, overseeing the purchase of a wide range of products and services for SAP.  Whether procuring IT hardware or automobiles for the car fleet, the team uses SAP’s suite of procurement solutions, including SAP Ariba Category Management with AI, and often serves as an early adopter of new technology.

Category management is implemented by sourcing champions who play a critical role in optimizing spend and driving value. This approach delivers benefits across four main areas, helping to:

  1. Reduce system disruptions
  2. Support data-driven decision-making
  3. Ensure transparency and buy-in
  4. Review progress against objectives

SAP Ariba Category Management transforms the team’s ability to collect and analyze operational data, enrich it with external market information, and use built-in frameworks to strengthen recommendations for budget owners.

Asaad explains, "I’ve always made decisions based on my expertise, but the Category Management tool allows me to support these decisions with Porter's 5 Forces and the Kraljic Matrix. I know what I'm doing, but my budget owner needs to see that. The tool shows them the deep-dive analysis of the spend and the breakdown with the Kraljic Matrix, along with the market analysis using Porter's Five Forces. I've defined an initiative to help you, the budget owner, get better value for your money. With that you will get better stakeholder buy in, really showing how professionally we can optimize their spend and drive value."

The team tracks success and progress through KPIs like cost savings and supplier performance. By measuring and reporting on these metrics, they demonstrate procurement's impact on the bottom line and justify continued investment.

The Implementation and Change Management Process

The GPO’s journey demonstrates the power of thoughtful implementation and execution. From the initial three- to four-month rollout to ongoing usage, they've seen firsthand how strategic planning can drive transformative change.

To enhance awareness and adoption of the new solution, the team hosted several training sessions and worked closely with key stakeholders and budget owners. "We started category days where we brought the GPO organization together with key stakeholders, and each category presented their key strategy,” Asaad says. “Since then, we have done this on a six-month basis, showing the changes over time for each category and getting everybody onboard."

Through SAP Ariba Category Management with AI, the GPO can easily: 

  • Connect processes, data, and user experiences across systems
  • Share recommendations based on supplier fragmentation, price variances, tail spend, supplier diversity, and more
  • Make data-driven decisions with actionable insights and enriched supplier data
  • Ensure transparency and stakeholder buy-in to manage change proactively
  • Review progress against key objectives and clear metrics

AI enhances the team’s efficiency in understanding market dynamics and supporting optimal pricing strategies with key suppliers.

Adopting AI: Strategic Considerations

Many practitioners are hesitant to adopt AI due to lack of understanding or trust in the technology. "In my organization, 90% are really afraid of the solution and 10% are absolutely excited,” Asaad says. “It's changing the way we have to think in the future. The earlier you adopt, the better you will manage it."

And regarding whether organizations should implement AI solutions now or wait, Asaad advises, "You should have implemented it yesterday. If you wait until the tool is more mature, the challenges of adopting AI will increase. At this point, we're still smarter than the AI advancements and recommendations, so we can correct, interact, and learn and grow with the tool. If we wait, it will overwhelm us."

By starting now, teams can gradually develop AI expertise and help shape how the technology is integrated into key processes within the organization. Delaying adoption may result in a steeper learning curve and less control or understanding for how AI works.

 

Conclusion

By using SAP solutions, the GPO is not only optimizing procurement processes but also sharing valuable insights that drive product development and enhance customer value. This “SAP Runs SAP” approach demonstrates the real-world impact of our AI-powered procurement solutions and sets a high bar for excellence.

Listen to the Procurement Leaders webinar on demand to learn more about, Leveraging AI for Category Management.

entry.title: Change credit limit currency.
entry.content:
Credit limit currency is not as per the customer currency and not editable.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAC Story breaks when Datasphere model is updated
entry.content: Yes Hadar, unchecking an assoc in model-view, or removing / changing dimensions / measures will prompt for an warning -> stories will be affected.

entry.title: Re: Change credit limit currency.
entry.content:
Enter the required currency in the credit segment from SSCUI Create Credit Segments (ID 102104).

Refer SAP KBA 3599231 - Changing Credit Segment Currency in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: 1H 2025 Release - New export button on scheduled job manager
entry.content:
Hello SAP community!

I hope you"re doing well.

I’m reaching out regarding the new "Export" button now available on the Scheduled Job Manager page as part of the 1H 2025 Release.

Could you please provide more details on how this functionality works? I tested it and noticed that it appears to export all jobs rather than allowing the selection of a specific job to export. I would appreciate clarification on what information is included in the export and if there are any options available to customize or filter the exported data.

Understanding this will help us better adapt to the new functionality and ensure we are using it effectively.

Thank you very much for your support!

entry.title: Re: CMS SSL config and thick clients
entry.content: We have an internal CA which permits us to create two-year certificates which reduces the frequency of renewing the certificates. However, I agree. It does add to the maintenance tasks for maintaining the BusinessObjects software.

entry.title: Re: Library view - price and item description
entry.content: Different classes or segments can override item price and be different for each & alternatively different libraries can have the separate costs associated and a user can have multiple library access with varying prices on each and users had the potential for different currencies as well, so the prices can't be shown only with one without the label properly, but you can remove the label, see 2597029 - Remove label for estimated price

entry.title: Re: Optional Sandbox system starter price
entry.content:
@JAS3311 ,

Just add a few points on what my colleague Bob said:

- To keep the Starter System after you get the Production Tenant, you need to notify SAP, especially your AE, right away. Because after 30 (?) days of lock your Starter System (that's 60 days after production tenant provision), your Starter System will be gone completely. So you want to make sure SAP's ops team is aware of your intention.

- There is a cost to keep the Starter System, and it is usually paid for by your AE. That means you really need a justification for him/her to pay that bill for you.

- Besides Starter System, do you consider a Sandbox System, which is fully paid by you as a customer. You should contact your AE as well for this option. 

Hope these points help.

Regards,

George

entry.title: Re: Evolution of Pricing in SAP
entry.content: nice explaination Pranav

entry.title: Re: SAP IAS Federated Access AWS Cognito
entry.content:
Hi Diego,

In general there isn't much in the setup, even though it sounds complex. 🙂

You exchange metadata between the 2- AWS and IAS. Basically IAS metadata will be exported and used in AWS to create an app and AWS metadata will be exported and will be used in IAS to create an application. Once this is done, the trust is established between AWS and IAS.

Once that is done, you have 2 options depending on whether AWS is your IDP or you want IAS to be the IDP

1. if AWS is the IDP then you can just set IAS as a proxy using conditional authentication. so when the request comes from the user, IAS just passes that request to AWS for authentication etc. This is the simplest approach and all you need to do is map parameters (for e.g. first name, last name, user name etc.) in AWS in the app that was created for IAS.

2. If however you want IAS to be the IDP going forward, then there is much more involved where you need to sync or create users in IAS which will then be used as the Idp for user authentication for the app. You will also have to adjust/ map certain parameters (for e.g. first name, last name, user name etc.) between IAS and your end user application.

I hope this gives you some insight in the setup. 

Julius

P.S: The booster did just that. It setup trust between your IAS and the BTP subaccount where the build workzone service resides.

entry.title: Kanban Replenishment strategy - stock transfer - delivery from an EWM storage location
entry.content:
Dear All,

I have the following customer requirement:

For a specific control cycle with stock transfer control and integrated EWM (Extended Warehouse Management), when the control cycle is set to 'empty,' the system automatically generates a delivery in EWM.

When the goods receipt is executed, the status is changed to 'Complete', or 'FULL'.

Currently, the process works correctly. However, we’d like the system to automatically set the control cycle status to 'empty' once stock for a specific SUPPLY AREA is fully consumed. Is this achievable? We’ve tested both classic and event-based control cycles option.


Classic:

Event:

entry.title: Re: FG Connector Library Documentation incorrect

entry.title: Restricted Access to Pricing Configuration Elements in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
I am a Junior Consultant for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, specializing in the Sales and Distribution module. I am still becoming familiar with the system, and I am currently reviewing the topic of sales pricing configuration. When I try to access the app Implementation Activities -> General Business Data -> Price Management -> Sales Pricing -> Setting for Pricing in Sales, I only see a few configuration options available, and they do not include the basic settings needed for condition configuration, such as condition tables, pricing procedures, or access sequences.

When I try to check these configurations through CBC, I am redirected to an error page.
I have confirmed with my facilitator that I have the necessary access rights.
Do you know what might be causing this issue?

Is it possible to modify the pick pack and production manager form, addin a new column to the grid? I mean, using UI API. One of our customer needs to add a new column to calculate some values. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Monitor Bank Fees app Camt.086 AFP service Service Codes calculated volume field
entry.content:
Thanks for your response BulentD,
I am getting below error message while maintaining the external codes in "Manage Banks" under the tab Bank Service Mapping.
Transaction type 40000200, external transaction FEE assigned to service 40000200FCLM_BAM_MAINT2/036.In OT83 we just created as below 

,
Can you please guide me how to proceed further , SAP Replied we need to check our self as it is custom transfermation.

entry.title: Re: LOAD TABLE syntax
entry.content:
The syntax for ROW LOG is 

| ROW LOG log-target
log-target : {
FILE server-filename
| CLIENT FILE client-filename
| VARIABLE variable-name
}

You are missing FILE or CLIENT FILE ( or VARIABLE) in your statement.  See line 4 below:

load table t1 (c1,c2) 
    using file 'd:\\u.txt' 
    allow all errors 
    row log file 'd:\\r.lg' 
    delimited by ';'

entry.title: SAP Fiori (F3074A) Timesheet V4 Worklist Enhancement
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am currently working with Timesheet V4 application and currently trying to extend the Worklist tab (available via Assignment --> Worklist). The idea is to add description to the given value. E.g,. the column for Task Level (standard column enabled via customizing and populated via BADi - HRFIO_IF_TSH_ENRICH~ENRICH_WORKLIST). The column contains key value only, e.g., Z010 but I would want to show it in a form Z010 (Preparation) or (Preparation) Z010.

What would be the steps or where should I navigate to in order to investigate / implement the case? I have no possibility to create extension project via BAS / Build code and have S4 / CDS and BADIs as options in my hands.

The help / advise would be highly appreciated!

Br,
Aleks.
? should be replaced with text of Z010

Tarkan

entry.title: Express document "Update was terminated" received from author
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We are using 'BAPI_PO_CHANGE' to modify Delivery date, some times after 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT' we are getting error “Express document "Update was terminated" received from author”. If we run same file second time it will get processed successfully.

 

Can anyone please let me know how to suppress this error.

 

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_PO_CHANGE'
    EXPORTING
      PURCHASEORDER                = T_EKET-EBELN
*     POHEADER                     = T_HEADER
*     POHEADERX                    = T_HEADERX
*     POADDRVENDOR                 =
*     TESTRUN                      =
*     MEMORY_UNCOMPLETE            =
*     MEMORY_COMPLETE              =
*     POEXPIMPHEADER               =
*     POEXPIMPHEADERX              =
*     VERSIONS                     =
*     NO_MESSAGING                 =
*     NO_MESSAGE_REQ               =
*     NO_AUTHORITY                 =
*     NO_PRICE_FROM_PO             =
*   IMPORTING
*     EXPHEADER                    =
*     EXPPOEXPIMPHEADER            =
   TABLES
     RETURN                       = RETURN
*     POITEM                       = T_POITEM
*     POITEMX                      = T_POITEMX
*     POADDRDELIVERY               = T_POADDRDELIVERY
     POSCHEDULE                   = T_POSCHEDULE
     POSCHEDULEX                  = T_POSCHEDULEX
*     POACCOUNT                    =
*     POACCOUNTPROFITSEGMENT       =
*     POACCOUNTX                   =
*     POCONDHEADER                 =
*     POCONDHEADERX                =
*     POCOND                       = T_POCOND
*     POCONDX                      =
*     POLIMITS                     =
*     POCONTRACTLIMITS             =
*     POSERVICES                   =
*     POSRVACCESSVALUES            =
*     POSERVICESTEXT               =
*     EXTENSIONIN                  =
*     EXTENSIONOUT                 = T_EXTENSIONOUT
*     POEXPIMPITEM                 =
*     POEXPIMPITEMX                =
*     POTEXTHEADER                 =
*     POTEXTITEM                   =
*     ALLVERSIONS                  =
*     POPARTNER                    = T_POPPARTNER
*     POCOMPONENTS                 =
*     POCOMPONENTSX                =
*     POSHIPPING                   =
*     POSHIPPINGX                  =
*     POSHIPPINGEXP                =
*     POHISTORY                    =
*     POHISTORY_TOTALS             =
*     POCONFIRMATION               =
            .
  READ TABLE RETURN  WITH KEY TYPE = 'E' .
  IF SY-SUBRC IS INITIAL.
    CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_ROLLBACK'
*     IMPORTING
*       RETURN        =
              .
    DELETE RETURN WHERE TYPE NE 'E'.
    LOOP AT RETURN.
      IT_ERROR-MESS = RETURN-MESSAGE.
      IT_ERROR-PO = T_EKET-EBELN.
      APPEND IT_ERROR.
    ENDLOOP.
  ELSE.
    READ TABLE RETURN  WITH KEY TYPE = 'A' .
    IF SY-SUBRC IS INITIAL.
      CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_ROLLBACK'.             .
      DELETE RETURN WHERE TYPE NE 'A'.                                             
      LOOP AT RETURN.
        IT_ERROR-MESS = RETURN-MESSAGE.
        IT_ERROR-PO = T_EKET-EBELN.
        APPEND IT_ERROR.
      ENDLOOP.
    ELSE.
      CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
      EXPORTING
        WAIT          = 'X'.
 
ENDIF.

entry.title: Re: Error : Executing CAP with SAP HANA Cloud project as MTA
entry.content:
Thanks for the response

I am following these steps: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/hana-cloud-cap-deploy-mta.html

And yes the url is included, even with a more generic one, please see image bellow.

The example is a project from SAP, so this is the github for it: https://github.com/YojanPeru050704/demoCAPHana

If I try to access srv url, it returns 401, might be correct.

But the app url e54aba11trial-dev-democaphana.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com/interaction_items/webapp/index.html

returns 500,  The log includes this message - no windows to prompt for credentials is shown:

4caef6c9ac5","space_name":"dev","space_id":"753c88af-e37e-405c-8fc4-ca4fe7fb36d2","container_id":"10.32.1.8","msg":"Illegal content in cookie: cmapi_cookie_privacy, you may want to set environment variable COOKIE_BACKWARD_COMPATIBILITY to true for backward compatible validation, argument val is invalid: permit 1 required","level":"error","written_at":"2025-04-28T17:28:49.457Z","written_ts":1745861329457940200,"request_id":"cfd1f25e-e408-43e7-5e32-556500ccbf44","correlation_id":"cfd1f25e-e408-43e7-5e32-556500ccbf44","tenant_id":"19e48778-6e5e-405d-adcb-e65a6b9eda1c"}

https://e54aba11trial-dev-democaphana-srv.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com/odata/v4/catalog/Countries

I ran out of ideas for this deployment

Thank you for any guidance.

entry.title: Global Currency amount is 0, how to correct it?
entry.content:
The global currency amount in JE should be the same as company code currency amount. How can I correct it?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Global Currency amount is 0, how to correct it?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community, 

 

To correct a journal entry where the global currency amount is 0, you should reverse the transfer document and post it again with the correct parallel currency values. This issue can occur if you specified not to translate values automatically and did not enter historical values, resulting in a zero amount being posted. Reversing and reposting with accurate values will resolve the error.

 

You can also refer to below Documentation for details:

Reversing Journal Entries

 

I hope this helps!

 

Best Regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Unable to send the observation content via email
entry.content:
I am not able to  send the observation content via email to the observer when accessing the observation details through learning. I am trying by Send Email Notification Button 

Do you know if is it possible?

Thank you

Hi All,

I would like to see a report generated from buyer login which contains PR and PO numbers that has ben created using Guided buying.

Can you please let me know how to get a report?

entry.title: Re: Purchase Order Accruals Review - All accruals are rev...
entry.content:
Hi @Gabor5, Thanks for the feedback! You’re right, that earlier link doesn’t open properly. To find the official SAP guide, 

To find the official SAP guide, please:

  1. Go to SAP Help Portal

  2. Search for: "2VB Purchase Order Accruals"

  3. Open the Finance – Purchase Order Accruals section.

  4. Then look for topics like Review Status, Thresholds, and Workflow Settings.

Regards,

Chuma

entry.title: Custom PPF action in S/4 HANA Public Cloud TM
entry.content:
Hello,

from FO, we need to send an email to carrier with BOL attached as PDF. Is it possible in standard S/4 HANA Public cloud TM ?  or if custom PPF action is required, i cannot see any config path for defining custom PPF action.

I dont know what the below settings does ?

rgds

sar

entry.title: Migration of Accounting Transaction Data tool be used to update existing fixed assets data
entry.content:
Can the Migration of Accounting Transaction Data tool be used to update existing fixed assets data? if not, is there a migration template that can?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Migration of Accounting Transaction Data tool be used...
entry.content:
From 2408 release, it is possible to mass upload Depreciation, Revaluation, Acquisition, a Partial Transfer of IMAT or a Complete Transfer of IMAT. The template to perform the mass upload do not have a Target Fixed Asset ID field if you would like to perform a complete transfer. 

From below link, download the XML template https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2754875d2d2a403f95e58a41a9c7d6de/2cbd61a1722d10149831d725ceb6610b.html

In the Fixed Asset Accounting entry Mass Upload - XML File Generation Template. In regards to Fixed asset ID fields, there is the "Mandatory: Fixed Asset Master ID" and the "Optional: Fixed Asset ID".

So this is the only feature available in the mass upload of fixed assets.

entry.title: Fiscal Period appear as: 9999-12 in the Report: Production Variance Details.
entry.content:
Why does Fiscal Period appear as: 9999-12 in the Report: Production Variance Details ?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Fiscal Period appear as: 9999-12 in the Report: Produ...
entry.content: The Period format is generated correctly and the system behaves as expected. It is not an issue, and this is the standard date format configurations in the product variance details report.

entry.title: Unable to change currency in Purchase order.
entry.content:
You were unable to change the currency in Purchase order from EUR to USD which is in sent status.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Unable to change currency in Purchase order.
entry.content: Once the Purchase order is in sent status, It is not possible to change the data in the currency field hence that filed should be already greyed out. Ensure to correct the currency before making purchase order in sent status.

entry.title: Re: No Userexits in SUSG/SCMON Data
entry.content: @OSO_MH I don't know the standard SAP Custom Code Migration app. Probably that SAP programs can do exactly as humans do, i.e. see that the include ZXLOIU02 is used by only the function module EXIT_SAPLLOI1_002, which is not used, so propose to remove ZXLOIU02. It's what I would do.

entry.title: Secure JWT Signing and Generation with RSA in SAP Cloud Integration (CI)
entry.content:
In this blog, you'll discover how to set up an SAP Cloud Integration (CI) iFlow that generates OAuth 2.0 access tokens using JWT, focusing on robust security implementations.

If you're interested in less complex security setups—such as symmetric algorithms, shared client secrets, and manual encoding of headers and claims to base64—you might want to check out SAP Cloud Integration: Bloomberg API Integration Using JWT–OAuth. It offers alternative approaches using symmetric encryption.

If you're aiming for higher security, including the use of RSA algorithms involving a private key for signing and a public key for verification, as well as JWT construction utilizing the io.jsonwebtoken library, please continue reading.

JWT Overview:

JSON Web Tokens are essentially compact, URL-safe tokens that carry claims between two parties. Each JWT is composed of three parts:

  • Header: Contains metadata about the token, such as its type and the algorithm used.
  • Payload: This section holds the claims, providing information like user identity and other relevant data.
  • Signature: Validates the integrity and authenticity of the token's contents.

JWTs are favored for being stateless and secure, streamlining the authentication process efficiently.

Introducing JWTs in SAP Cloud Integration (CI):

Implementing JWTs within SAP Cloud Integration (CI) can elevate your authentication strategy, offering robust methods for session management and API interactions. Since there's no standard way to generate JWTs in SAP Cloud Integration (CI), using Groovy scripts becomes essential for creating them within integration flows. This blog focuses on a secure iFlow design tailored for generating these tokens with advanced security measures.

High Level Design:

Flows can be designed/orchestrated based on the integration requirement. In my case, based on the requirement, I have orchestrated the flows as depicted below.

 

iFlow Design - Component Breakdown:

Following are the iFlows involved-

  1. Specific iFlow - To receive and post data
  2. Generic iFlow - To get Access Token

Specific iFlow - To receive and post data:

This iFlow consists of the following:

  • Integration Process:
    • Sender Channel: To receive the request from the sender system
    • Process Call 1: To Call Get Access Token Flow
    • Process Call 2: To call Receiver Flow

Under Runtime Configuration, Allow access_token under Allowed Header(s)

  • Get Access Token (Local Integration Process):
    • Content Modifier: To set the scope which specific to API which is being called from receiver system
    • Request Reply: To call Generic Access Token Flow
  • Receiver Flow (Local Integration Process):
    • Content Modifier: To set the received access token from the generic flow as Authorization bearer while authenticating the receiver API
    • Request - Reply: To call Receiver System API

Generic iFlow – To get Access Token:

  • Sender Channel: Configure Process Direct to receive the request from the specific iFlow.
  • Content Modifier: To set headers and exchange properties which are required for JWT generation in the next step

I have maintained client_id, token_url and subject in the iFlow which can be externalized. private_key and certificate (public) is stored in the keystore and its alias name is maintained here.

  • Groovy Script: To generate JWT
import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import com.sap.it.api.ITApiFactory
import com.sap.it.api.keystore.KeystoreService
import io.jsonwebtoken.Jwts
import io.jsonwebtoken.SignatureAlgorithm
import java.math.BigDecimal
import java.security.KeyFactory
import java.security.PrivateKey
import java.security.cert.CertificateFactory
import java.security.cert.X509Certificate
import java.security.spec.PKCS8EncodedKeySpec
import java.util.Base64
import java.util.Map

def Message processData(Message message) {
    Map<String, Object> map = message.getProperties()
    def clientId = map.get("client_id")
    def tokenUrl = map.get("token_url")
    def privateKeyAlias = map.get("private_key")
    def certificateAlias = map.get("certificate")
    def subject = map.get("subject") ?: "CN=xxxxxxx, O=Demo, L=xxxx, S=xxxx, C=xx"

    KeystoreService keystoreService = ITApiFactory.getService(KeystoreService.class, null)
    if (keystoreService == null) {
        throw new IllegalStateException("Failed to access KeystoreService")
    }

    PrivateKey privateKey = keystoreService.getKey(privateKeyAlias)
    X509Certificate certificate = keystoreService.getCertificate(certificateAlias)

    byte[] privateKeyBytes = privateKey.getEncoded()
    PKCS8EncodedKeySpec keySpec = new PKCS8EncodedKeySpec(privateKeyBytes)
    KeyFactory keyFactory = KeyFactory.getInstance("RSA")
    PrivateKey rsaPrivateKey = keyFactory.generatePrivate(keySpec)

    def certificateString = Base64.getEncoder().encodeToString(certificate.getEncoded())
    List<String> x5c = [certificateString]

    def jti = UUID.randomUUID().toString().replaceAll("-", "")[0..21]
    def iat = new BigDecimal(System.currentTimeMillis() / 1000).setScale(0, BigDecimal.ROUND_DOWN)
    def exp = new BigDecimal(System.currentTimeMillis() / 1000 + 900).setScale(0, BigDecimal.ROUND_DOWN)

    Map<String, Object> claims = [
        iss: clientId,
        aud: tokenUrl,
        sub: subject,
        jti: jti,
        exp: exp,
        iat: iat,
        nbf: iat,
    ]

    String jwtToken = Jwts.builder()
        .setHeaderParam("typ", "JWT")
        .setHeaderParam("alg", "RS256")
        .setHeaderParam("x5c", x5c)
        .setClaims(claims)
        .signWith(SignatureAlgorithm.RS256, rsaPrivateKey)
        .compact()

    message.setProperty("jwt", jwtToken)
    return message
}

Following are done in this script

  1. Property Handling: Gathers IDs and aliases for token generation.
  2. Keystore Integration: Retrieves RSA keys and certificates for signing the JWT.
  3. JWT Formation: Sets up token headers, claims, and signs with RSA.
  4. Token Storage: Holds onto the JWT for later use.
  • Content Modifier: To set HTTP Request Body with the grant_type, assertion and scope (from the specific flow)
  • Request – Reply: Call Receiver system and get the access token

For enhanced security, let’s dive into critical considerations:

RSA Implementation

  1. Private Key Handling: Ensure secure storage of private keys, while public keys can be distributed for verification.
  2. Increased Security: RS256 enhances protection with double-key mechanisms suited for distributed environments.
  3. Regulatory Compliance: RSA often satisfies stringent compliance needs due to its robust security architecture.

 Testing:

Whenever the sender system sends data to be posted in receiver system

  1. Specific iFlow will receive the request and calls the Generic iFlow to get Access Token

2. Generic iFlow will generate JWT, connects to Receiver system and gets the Access Token which is shared back to the Specific iFlow

3. With this Access Token specific flow will be able to post the data to Receiver System API

To sum it up, while SAP Cloud Integration doesn't natively support JWT generation, the use of Groovy scripts fills this crucial gap, allowing your integrations to utilize secure tokens effectively. Whether you're after a straightforward implementation or seeking the higher security standards provided by RSA, this approach gives you flexibility and control over your authentication processes. By adopting these strategies, you are not just enhancing security but you are setting up your API interactions in a way that's prepared to meet today's demanding standards while paving the way for scalable, reliable integrations.

 Additional Resources

  • SAP Cloud Integration (CI) Groovy Scripting: help document
  • Learning JWT: https://jwt.io/introduction
  • Understanding OAuth: link

While these integration flows are robust enough to generate JWTs and obtain access tokens from APIs, there's always room for improvement and optimization. My goal in this blog was to provide you with a foundational understanding of how JWTs can be crafted and utilized within SAP Cloud Integration. I hope this gives you a solid starting point for integrating secure authentication into your projects. If you have any feedback or questions, I'd love to hear from you. Please feel free to share your thoughts in the comment section below!

entry.title: Re: Eclipse ADT: impossible to add a breakpoint in ABAP s...
entry.content: Not a priority of course but that would nice, thank you!

entry.title: Re: Express document "Update was terminated" received from author
entry.content: Check SAP notes. I find a few ones for "j_3avasso" "duplicate".

entry.title: Re: Client Copy in SAP ECC vs. SAP S/4HANA: Key Differences
entry.content: Overall it's a very good blog, well explained in very simple terms and comprehensive. Could you please provide some real-time metrics (tables, sizes, times etc.) ?

entry.title: Re: Completed Learning Activities status showing as Planned
entry.content:
We see this as well when the employees add a learning goal AFTER they have already completed it. The two systems do work together, but the developmental goal needs to be set first in order for it to show complete. Employees need to set the goal prior to completing the training for it to show complete.

The functionality is similar to if you directly assign a training to an employee - if they already completed and then it gets re-assigned, the system assumes it needs to be completed again.

 In these cases, I have recommended for them to delete the new goal and just make a notation of the completion manually instead.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA activating account based CO-PA parallely to costing based CO-PA (brownfield)
entry.content: Hi Nsk! What did you do in the end, did you activate both? We have a similar situation

entry.title: Extend the Standard field "Customer-specific Text" to the template G/L account balances.
entry.content:
You want to know the process on how to extend the Standard field "Customer-specific Text" to the template G/L account balances.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Extend the Standard field "Customer-specific Text" to...
entry.content:
Any new field which you are trying to add in the standard migration template which can be achieved through the updation of extension fields.

To do that follow the steps mentioned in this KBA: https://me.sap.com/notes/1842942

entry.title: Re: BTP Trial Account: Cloud Connector disconnected
entry.content: Update: Whatever was the cause it seems to have 'fixed itself'. No longer getting an error for the same call.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Security: How to handle Authorization and Attributes [2] with XSUAA and IAS
entry.content:
@CarlosRoggan very cool blog post! Thanks for the nice explanations. You clearly put lots of effort into it 🙂 

Are there any best practices on how to use the IAS attributes for typical SAP org-units like plants or company code? Imagine a BTP app that shows master data which is plant or company code related. There are 3 users in IAS that are supposed to use the app.

User 1: has authorisation for plant 1010
User 2: has authorisation for plant 1710
User 3: has authorisation for plants 1010 and 1710

Is there a best practice approach to how to use the User Attributes from the Identity Directory for this requirement?

entry.content: Comunidad alguno de ustedes maneja sus incapacidades laborales un SuccessFactors, de que forma? en un portlet customizado? como manejan las incapacidades subsecuentes y el manejo de los primeros 3 dias ?

entry.title: Re: Notification days after Hire Date
entry.content:
Hi,

Did you happen to solve this issue? I am dealing with the same problem.

Any solution?

Thank you!

Szilvia

entry.title: Signature Verification and Missing Resources Error Building MDK on iOS - Xcode Build Failed
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am encountering critical issues while trying to build my MDK application on iOS. I am getting the following error during the Xcode build process, which is preventing me from moving forward:

There seems to be a serious issue with the signature verification and essential resources missing in several SAP xcframeworks. Has anyone encountered this error before, or does anyone have any insights into what might be causing this and how to resolve it? I'm quite stuck with this problem.

Any help or suggestions you can offer would be greatly appreciated. Thanks in advance!

SAP Mobile Services SAP BTP SDK for iOS iOS

entry.title: Re: Delivery Completion Indicator PO item - MM_PUR_S4_PO_...
entry.content:
Hello, 

In SAP Help documentation you can find following information:

With this BAdI, you can

  • Set or change values of custom fields in purchase orders on item level and make changes to the following standard fields:

    TAXCODE, TAXCOUNTRY, INCOTERMSCLASSIFICATION, INCOTERMSTRANSFERLOCATION, INCOTERMSLOCATION1, INCOTERMSLOCATION2,SHIPPINGTYPE, PURCHASINGINFORECORDUPDATECODE

    Note

    PURCHASINGINFORECORDUPDATECODE is an optional parameter and the available values are:

    FixedShort Description

     

    No update

    A

    Update with or without plant

    B

    Update with plant (if no plant ban)

    C

    Update without plant (if no plant requirement)

  • Use the optional importing parameter: PURCHASEORDERITEM_OLD. This parameter contains the item data of the previous roundtrip, for example, the data before the last change was done.

  • Raise messages by filling the changing parameter table MESSAGES

Note

If neither BAdI Modify Purchase Order Header nor BAdI Modify Purchase Order Item is implemented, the custom fields will be open for input when creating or changing purchase orders and will be in read-only mode when displaying purchase orders.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/af9ef57f504840d2b81be8667206d485/4a3cd87985414d2c97ffa6b2ff4f0ab9.html?locale=en-US

So parameter PURCHASEORDERITEM is not modifible field in this BAdI.

Could you please specifie the result you want to achive? You need Delivery Completion Indicator to be set at item level after meeting certain requirements?

Regards, 

Jagoda Drwiega

entry.title: Re: Display the number of installments in the supplier item
entry.content: Hi Chuma. Thank you for your quick reply about this subject. What I need is a way to set the vendor line items at the accounting document numbered. Would be possible to set any configuration that would order the BSEG-BUZEI? So first would come any number of vendors line item and tham it would be followed by any other posting item? Eg. 001 – Vendor XXX; 200 USD 002 – Vendor XXX; 200 USD 003 – Vendor XXX; 200 USD 004 – Taxes 005 – Interest 006 - Others

entry.title: Re: Schedule Contact Email tag in SystemEnrollmentNotific...
entry.content:
Hi @pv221519 

Can you make a test?

  1. At System Administration > Configuration > Global Application Settings > Mail tab >
  2. Uncheck "Always use Application Admin's email address as the From address on Vcalendar attachments" > click on 'Apply Changes' >
  3. Now trigger the SystemEnrollmentNotification again for the sample class.

What's the value that gets populated for the 'Schedule Contact Email' tag?

entry.title: Posting a Job Vacancy for a Specific Sub-Entity
entry.content:
Hi community!

Is it possible to post a job vacancy specifically for employees of a particular sub-entity? For example, if the company has multiple sub-entities, can we target the job posting to only one of them?

entry.title: Re: Depreciation expense decreasing the budget loaded in...
entry.content:
Hi,

You can change the assignment to cost collectors (asset time-depending data) at any point in time regardless the asset was capitalized already or not. This WBS element has nothing to do with origin WBS assigned to an AuC. In that case no posting occurs to WBS (alos not statistically).  

Posting depreciation to a locked or close cost object will result in an error during asset depreciation run.

entry.title: Re: Restricted Access to Pricing Configuration Elements i...
entry.content:
Hi,

 Do you have authorizations to other configuration elements? Can you please check? 

Regards

Jai Srinivasan

entry.title: Interview Scheduling for Experts
entry.content:
Hi community!

Currently, the system allows candidates to accept or reject interview dates or slots. Within the integration with MS Outlook and Teams, is it possible to replace the candidate with interview experts for scheduling purposes?"

entry.title: Re: Proration and Number Formats in Variable Pay
entry.content: Hi Xavier - On a related note regarding proration, we have an issue with the FTE percent from EC.  We have an employee with an FTE rate of 93.33 in EC, but the history import only brings in .93.  Is it possible to bring 4 decimals into the history import?

entry.title: Candidate blacklist
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We have a blacklist database of candidates. Is it possible to configure the system to automatically block these candidates, based on a unique identifier (e.g., email or passport ID), and prevent them from applying to vacancies?

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps - Frustrated Newbie
entry.content: Thanks. This video helps.

entry.title: SAP DataServices setup automation
entry.content:
Is there a way to initiate following as non-interactive commands ? 

  1. Configure DSN with SSL
  2. Test Connection with CMC console
  3. Set up AccessServer
  4. Setup JobServer
  5. Define RT services and instances from DS Management console
  6. Configure WebServices from DS Management Console

entry.title: Issue adding a note or reference to a payment item via plugin using addPayoutItems in SAP CCO
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I am working with SAP Customer Checkout (CCO) and have encountered an issue when trying to add a note or reference to a payment item using a custom plugin.

The logic is implemented in the after event of the addPayoutItems function, where we attempt to insert the note or reference dynamically.
Specifically, we followed a similar development approach used when cancelling an item, where a new list of items is generated — including the updated one — and then the receipt and the ReceiptManager are updated accordingly.

While the plugin correctly adds the modified list and calls the expected update functions, the note or reference is not being saved or reflected on the final payment item when validating the transaction through the manager.

Screenshots will be attached to illustrate:

  • How the information is injected during the addPayoutItems event.

     

  • The result after validation where the note/reference is missing.

Has anyone experienced something similar or can provide guidance?
Is there an additional step required to persist changes to payment items when modifying them through a plugin?

Thanks in advance for your support!

entry.title: Re: Fiori-Style Analytics Dashboard: 'Invalid Username/Pa...
entry.content:
hello agian , 

i apologize for yesterday it is looks like the handling of users in keycloak was not that easy job , so i have recompiled jar files that in charge of username and post them .. here is the complete guide for best results : 

download my new compiled keycloak_tolowercase.tar.gz from here : 

https://github.com/maenmustafa/keycloak/blob/main/keycloak_tolowercase.tar.gz

please it in any folder .. 

then extract it .. 
tar -xvf keycloak_tolowercase.tar.gz
go to the original folder and  backup the original jar files : 
cd /usr/sap/SAPBusinessOne/Common/keycloak/lib/lib/main

 

cp     org.keycloak.keycloak-crypto-default-24.0.4.jar    org.keycloak.keycloak-crypto-default-24.0.4.jar.bak
cp     org.keycloak.keycloak-model-jpa-24.0.4.jar    org.keycloak.keycloak-model-jpa-24.0.4.jar.bak
cp     org.keycloak.keycloak-model-storage-24.0.4.jar    org.keycloak.keycloak-model-storage-24.0.4.jar.bak
cp     org.keycloak.keycloak-model-storage-private-24.0.4.jar    org.keycloak.keycloak-model-storage-private-24.0.4.jar.bak
cp     org.keycloak.keycloak-server-spi-24.0.4.jar    org.keycloak.keycloak-server-spi-24.0.4.jar.bak
cp     org.keycloak.keycloak-server-spi-private-24.0.4.jar    org.keycloak.keycloak-server-spi-private-24.0.4.jar.bak
cp     org.keycloak.keycloak-services-24.0.4.jar    org.keycloak.keycloak-services-24.0.4.jar.bak

then back to the orignal folder where the jar files exist 


cp org.keycloak* /usr/sap/SAPBusinessOne/Common/keycloak/lib/lib/main

back to keycloak directory : 
cd /usr/sap/SAPBusinessOne/Common/keycloak/lib/lib/main
 
 
 
chmod 775     org.keycloak.keycloak-crypto-default-24.0.4.jar
chmod 775      org.keycloak.keycloak-model-jpa-24.0.4.jar
chmod 775      org.keycloak.keycloak-model-storage-24.0.4.jar
chmod 775      org.keycloak.keycloak-model-storage-private-24.0.4.jar
chmod 775      org.keycloak.keycloak-server-spi-24.0.4.jar
chmod 775      org.keycloak.keycloak-server-spi-private-24.0.4.jar
chmod 775      org.keycloak.keycloak-services-24.0.4.jar


chown b1service0:b1service0     org.keycloak.keycloak-crypto-default-24.0.4.jar
chown b1service0:b1service0      org.keycloak.keycloak-model-jpa-24.0.4.jar
chown b1service0:b1service0      org.keycloak.keycloak-model-storage-24.0.4.jar
chown b1service0:b1service0      org.keycloak.keycloak-model-storage-private-24.0.4.jar
chown b1service0:b1service0      org.keycloak.keycloak-server-spi-24.0.4.jar
chown b1service0:b1service0      org.keycloak.keycloak-server-spi-private-24.0.4.jar
chown b1service0:b1service0      org.keycloak.keycloak-services-24.0.4.jar

 

Login to Authentication Service

https://ServerIP_Or_Name:40020/auth/admin/sapb1/console/ 

login with B1SiteUser and password and then go to users 

.. delete all users EXCEPT b1siteuser

 

now restart the services

service sapb1servertools-authentication restart


the login to sap business one and the problem is solved.

 

Regards

Maen

entry.title: Hiring for Multiple Positions from a Single Requisition
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

Currently, when we need to hire multiple candidates based on a single job posting, we have to create multiple separate requisitions. We need to create, for example, 10 separate positions and link them all to a single requisition, so that during the hiring process, each candidate can be assigned to one of the available (vacant) positions. However, this process can become very labor-intensive, especially when we need to hire 60 to 100 candidates under one job posting. Considering that using Mass Positions is not an option for us (as 1 FTE = 1 employee), what would be the most efficient way to manage this process in SuccessFactors, both from a system configuration and operational workflow perspective?

entry.title: Re: Cannot find required duplicate file for exercise - Su...
entry.content:
Greetings.

In response to your first query it appears that you're attempting to perform exercise 5 ( "Working With The SQL Trace Tool" ).  The template ( for copying purposes ) for that exercise is "/LRN/CL_S4D401_ATS_PROFILING".  The "/LRN/CL_S4D401_ATS_SQL_TRACE" template you referred to is actually the solution not the template.  If you use the keyboard shortcut "CTRL+SHIFT+A" and type in the name the ADT should find it.

In response to your second question when creating a new ABAP class interfaces can be added in the "New ABAP Class" popup screen by clicking the "Add..." button towards the bottom right hand corner of the popup screen.  When clicking on the "Add..." button a new popup appears where you can type in the name of the interface ( "if_oo_adt_classrun" in your case ).  After typing in the name hit the "Ok" button and continue creating your class.  If for whatever reason the interface is not added then you can manually add it to the code by simply typing "INTERFACES if_oo_adt_classrun." under "PUBLIC SECTION" in the editor.

entry.title: How to Configure EAN 13 and EAN 14 Codes with External Number Assignment in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I would like to request your guidance on how to configure the EAN codes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public. I need to manage both EAN 13 and EAN 14 formats using external number assignment.

Could you please advise on how to set up and assign the appropriate number ranges? Additionally, I am not certain whether the EAN codes are assigned based on the material type or if the assignment is done more generally. If it is general, could you kindly explain where and how I should perform this configuration?

Thank you very much in advance for your support!

entry.title: Re: The 2024 Developer Insights Survey: The Report
entry.content: Terrific report and a great resource.  I'm glad the report mentions a future desire to break out the IDE choice preferences by language.  I hope the 2025 survey shows popular IDEs specifically for ABAP (SAPGUI vs Eclipse vs VSCode).  BTW the 2024 report seems to be missing from the main survey results page https://developers.sap.com/developer-insights-survey.html

entry.title: Re: Hybris user not authenticated after SAML successful r...
entry.content: @dprajeu I don't quite recall. Maybe it centered around the creation of the AuthenticationToken but I don't remember for sure. I was expecting the OOTB to do a seemless integration but it is not. There is a lot that you have to do on your own to make it work such as in the article link I provided.

entry.title: Re: Quick tip: How to check if the changes from the adaptation project are active?
entry.content: @RaminS, I have added it, thank you.

entry.title: Re: Schedule Contact Email tag in SystemEnrollmentNotific...
entry.content: https://me.sap.com/notes/0003600996

entry.title: Re: Restricted Access to Pricing Configuration Elements i...
entry.content: Hi Jai, Thank you for your reply. I have checked, and I do have access to several Sales Pricing configuration elements, including: Settings for Pricing in Sales Set Condition Types for Pricing in Sales Set Price List Types for Customers Set Price Groups for Customers Set Price Groups for Materials Define Price Maintenance Scenarios Settings for Taxes in Sales Maintain Tax Relevance for Customers Maintain Tax Relevance for Materials Maintain Determination of Value-Added Tax Registration Number However, the basic settings for condition tables, access sequences, and pricing procedures are not available for me to configure. When I attempt to access these configurations via CBC, I am redirected to an error page.
